target_population.description: Wave 2 eligible English-language online adult respondents who completed the somatic and ambient calibration instrument, consented to recontact when asked, and remain reachable for a 14-day retest administration.
target_population.geography: United States and District of Columbia
inclusion_criteria: Eligible usable Wave 2 complete with the v0.3.1 somatic and ambient item set.
No Wave 2 exclusion that invalidates score construction or respondent linkage.
Available protected panel identifier for deterministic Wave 2 to Wave 3 linkage.
Completes the retest survey in English within the preregistered retest window.
exclusion_criteria: Missing or nonlinkable Wave 2 respondent identifier.
Wave 2 record excluded after quality review or consent withdrawal.
Wave 3 duplicate, vendor-confirmed fraud, breakoff before usable item data, failed preregistered attention check, or completion outside the allowable retest window.
Evidence of study discussion, repeated unauthorized access, or other vendor-documented panel integrity violation.
coverage_limits: The analytic retest panel covers retained Wave 2 respondents only and does not cover new adults who were not part of the Wave 2 calibration frame.
Respondents lost to attrition, unreachable at recontact, or excluded by Wave 3 quality controls are outside the paired retest analytic sample.
Wave 3 supports repeatability and panel-conditioning checks for the v0.3.1 somatic score; it must not be used for standalone population prevalence, national trend, or event-study claims.

sampling_frame.frame_description: The retained recontact frame consists of Wave 2 eligible completes from the professional online panel with protected respondent linkage, Wave 2 final weights, item-level responses, consent status, and vendor reachability indicators.
known_undercoverage: Wave 2 nonrespondents and screenouts are not eligible for the retest frame.
Wave 2 completes without recontact permission or stable protected identifiers are excluded.
Attriters and unreachable respondents can differ from retained respondents on anxiety, AI exposure, and survey-engagement variables.
Refreshment respondents, if monitored, are not part of paired repeatability estimation.
sampling_frame.deduplication_rule: Use protected Wave 2 panel identifier as the primary linkage key, reject multiple Wave 3 completions for the same identifier except the first valid complete by timestamp, and remove vendor-confirmed fraud or duplicate device records before paired analysis.

recruitment_source.vendor_or_panel: Wave 2 professional online panel recontact sample
recruitment_source.recruitment_mode: Direct recontact invitation to eligible Wave 2 completes, fielded approximately 14 days after Wave 2 completion with no new AI capability event prompt.
recruitment_source.eligibility_screening: Screening confirms protected Wave 2 linkage, continued adult and US residence eligibility, English self-administration ability, consent, and completion within the preregistered retest window.
recruitment_source.nonresponse_tracking: Archive invited Wave 2 eligible respondents, undeliverable contacts, starts, refusals, breakoffs, completed retests, timing-window exclusions, quality exclusions, paired analytic cases, and attrition summaries by Wave 2 weighting and outcome covariates.

quota_variables.margin_source: Wave 2 eligible analytic respondent roster frozen before Wave 3 recontact
Wave 2 weighted and unweighted retained-panel margins
Wave 2 weighted and unweighted retained-panel margins
Wave 2 weighted and unweighted retained-panel margins
Wave 2 weighted and unweighted retained-panel margins
Wave 2 somatic_ambient_anxiety_mean quartiles computed before Wave 3 outcome inspection
Preregistered retest timing window distribution observed from fielding timestamps

planned_n_by_split.role: Paired retained-panel retest sample for 14-day repeatability estimation.
Optional nonpaired refreshment monitor for panel-conditioning checks only, excluded from ICC estimation.

construction_steps: Start from the Wave 2 final_weight for every linked retained-panel respondent as the base carryover weight.

Estimate a preregistered retention adjustment using Wave 2 demographic variables, Wave 2 score quartile, AI exposure, completion timestamp, and vendor reachability variables available before Wave 3 outcome inspection.
Calibrate retained-panel weights back to Wave 2 eligible analytic margins for age group, gender, race and ethnicity, education, Census region, and Wave 2 score quartile.
Do not use Wave 3 outcome responses to construct, trim, or diagnose weights before primary repeatability estimates are locked.
Assign refreshment monitor respondents separate refreshment weights for conditioning diagnostics only; never merge refreshment cases into paired ICC, mean-change, or item-stability estimates.
Trim retained-panel weights to the frozen bounds, rescale to mean one within the paired analytic sample, and map retest_final_weight to survey_weight on respondent-item rows.

survey_weight_mapping.mapping_rule: Copy paired respondent-level retest_final_weight to survey_weight on every Wave 3 respondent-item row after retention adjustment, calibration, trimming, and mean-one rescaling.

diagnostics: Wave 2 to Wave 3 retention rate overall and by each quota, monitoring, and Wave 2 score-quartile variable.
Attrition model coefficients, predicted-retention distribution, and covariate balance before and after retention adjustment.
Weight minimum, maximum, mean, median, coefficient of variation, effective sample size, design effect, and percent trimmed for paired retest cases.
Weighted and unweighted retained-panel distributions compared with the Wave 2 eligible analytic roster.
Refreshment monitor comparisons against retained-panel Wave 3 responses for panel-conditioning evidence, reported separately from repeatability estimates.

trim_bounds.exception_rule: Retest weights outside 0.25 to 4.00 are trimmed before rescaling; any wider bounds require a frozen addendum before Wave 3 outcome inspection and cannot be selected based on repeatability results.

variance_design.primary_standard_error: Respondent-level bootstrap resampling of paired retained respondents with retest_final_weight for ICC, mean-change, item-stability, attrition-adjusted sensitivity, and conditioning diagnostics.
variance_design.software: R psych, lme4, survey, and boot packages, versions locked in the Wave 3 retest analysis plan

subgroup_cells.suppression_rule: Report quartile-specific retention and repeatability descriptively only when each quartile has at least 75 paired respondents after exclusions.
Suppress age-specific repeatability summaries with fewer than 75 paired respondents or effective sample size below 50.
Suppress sparse gender categories with fewer than 40 paired respondents and prohibit confirmatory subgroup difference claims.
Report timing-window sensitivity only when each timing cell has at least 75 paired respondents; otherwise pool timing windows.
Use refreshment monitor cases only for conditioning diagnostics and never for paired repeatability or population prevalence estimates.

claim_limits.allowed_scope: Description of the retained Wave 2 to Wave 3 paired retest panel, attrition flow, refreshment monitor, and conditioning diagnostics.
claim_limits.limit_rationale: Retest interpretation requires transparent description of who was retained, lost, refreshed, or excluded.

claim_limits.allowed_scope: No standalone Wave 3 population prevalence or national level estimate is authorized.
claim_limits.limit_rationale: Wave 3 is a retained-panel retest of Wave 2 respondents and is designed for repeatability evidence, not a new cross-sectional population estimate.

claim_limits.allowed_scope: Descriptive repeatability summaries by prespecified retention, timing, and Wave 2 score strata when suppression rules and effective sample size checks pass.
claim_limits.limit_rationale: Subgroup summaries support robustness assessment for repeatability only and are not powered or designed for confirmatory subgroup difference testing.

claim_limits.allowed_scope: No population trend, longitudinal increase, longitudinal decrease, or repeated-cross-section change claim is authorized.
claim_limits.limit_rationale: The retest wave repeats the same construct over a short interval to assess measurement stability; observed mean change is a repeatability diagnostic only.

claim_limits.allowed_scope: No event-window, model-release shock, or causal event-study claim is authorized.
claim_limits.limit_rationale: The Wave 3 event registry is no_event and the design intentionally avoids interpreting retest differences as event effects.

claim_limits.allowed_scope: No causal effect claim is authorized from the retained-panel retest.
claim_limits.limit_rationale: Wave 3 contains no randomized or quasi-experimental exposure assignment and only estimates repeatability under recontact.

claim_limits.allowed_scope: No clinical, diagnostic, screening, treatment, or individual-risk interpretation is authorized.
claim_limits.limit_rationale: Retest reliability evidence does not convert ANX-Bench into a clinical or diagnostic measure.

claim_limits.allowed_scope: No respondent-level decision, ranking, eligibility, employment, clinical, or policy action may use Wave 3 scores.
claim_limits.limit_rationale: The retained-panel retest supports aggregate repeatability evidence only and cannot authorize decisions about individual respondents.
